heading: Examinations for merchant mariner credentials
label: 7510 Examinations for merchant mariner credentials

children.text: a question from any examination for a merchant mariner credential;
the answer to such a question, including any correct or incorrect answer that may be presented with such question; and

children.label: A
children.text: the manner in which such question has been, is, or may be selected for an examination;

children.label: B
children.text: the frequency of such selection; and

children.label: C
children.text: the frequency that an examinee correctly or incorrectly answered such question.

children.heading: .—Exception for Certain Questions
children.label: b .—Exception for Certain Questions
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a), the Secretary may, for the purpose of preparation by the general public for examinations required for merchant mariner credentials, release an examination question and answer that the Secretary has retired or is not presently on or part of an examination, or that the Secretary determines is appropriate for release.

children.label: A
children.text: 1 subject matter expert from the Coast Guard;

children.label: i
children.text: one-half shall be representatives from approved training facilities; and

children.label: ii
children.text: one-half shall be representatives from the appropriate maritime industry;

children.label: C
children.text: at least 1 representative from the National Merchant Marine Personnel Advisory Committee;

children.label: D
children.text: at least 2 representatives from the State maritime academies, of whom one shall be a representative from the deck training track and one shall be a representative of the engineer license track;

children.label: E
children.text: at least 2 individuals that have taken and passed the examination in the 5 years before the commissioning of the working group;

children.label: F
children.text: at least 1 representative from the United States Merchant Marine Academy;

children.label: G
children.text: representatives from other Coast Guard Federal advisory committees, as appropriate, for the industry segment associated with the subject examinations;

children.label: H
children.text: at least 1 subject matter expert from the Maritime Administration; and

children.label: I
children.text: at least 1 human performance technology representative.

children.heading: .—Inclusion of persons knowledgeable about examination type
children.label: 2 .—Inclusion of persons knowledgeable about examination type
children.text: The working group shall include representatives knowledgeable about the examination type under review.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Commandant shall convene the working group annually or at the creation of new examination questions, whichever occurs sooner.

children.heading: .—Remote participation
children.label: B .—Remote participation
children.text: The Commandant shall allow any member of the working group to participate remotely if the member of the working group does not have the means to participate in person.

children.heading: .—Use of questions
children.label: 4 .—Use of questions
children.text: The Commandant may not use questions developed for use in the Merchant Mariner Credentialing Examination until such questions are reviewed and approved by the working group.

children.label: i
children.text: industry standards, practices, and technology to be considered in the Merchant Mariner Credentialing Examination;

children.label: ii
children.text: the accuracy of examination questions;

children.label: iii
children.text: the accuracy and availability of examination references;

children.label: iv
children.text: the length of merchant mariner examinations;

children.label: v
children.text: the relevancy of examination topics and contents;

children.label: vi
children.text: any redundancy of core competencies between the Merchant Mariner Credentialing Examination and Standards of Training, Certification, and Watchingkeeping competencies; and

children.label: vii
children.text: the use of standard technologies in administering, scoring, and analyzing the examinations.

children.heading: .—Report to commandant
children.label: B .—Report to commandant
children.text: Upon completion of the review under this paragraph, a report shall be provided to the Commandant which shall include findings of the review with recommendations for updates to the Merchant Marine Credentialling Examination.

children.heading: .—Nondisclosure agreement
children.label: 6 .—Nondisclosure agreement
children.text: The Secretary shall require all members of the working group to sign a nondisclosure agreement with the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Treatment of members as federal employees
children.label: 7 .—Treatment of members as federal employees
children.text: A member of the working group who is not a Federal Government employee shall not be considered a Federal employee in the service or the employment of the Federal Government, except that such a member shall be considered a special government employee, as defined in for purposes of sections 203, 205, 207, 208, and 209 of such title and shall be subject to any administrative standards of conduct applicable to an employee of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating.section 202(a) of title 18

children.heading: 105.—Chapterof title
children.label: 8 105.—Chapterof title
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to any working group created under this section to review the Coast Guard’s merchant mariner credentialing examinations.

children.heading: .—Merchant Mariner Credential Defined
children.label: d .—Merchant Mariner Credential Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “merchant mariner credential” means a merchant mariner license, certificate, or document that the Secretary is authorized to issue pursuant to this title.
